features.name: price_per_kw
features.justification: Normalizes price by engine power so listings with similar performance are comparable; useful for value-for-money signals in regression.

features.name: log_price
features.justification: Price is right-skewed; log1p stabilizes variance and linearizes relationships for linear models.

features.name: vehicle_age
features.justification: Depreciation and wear correlate with age more than calendar year alone; reference_year should match the dataset era.

features.name: mileage_per_year
features.justification: Annualized usage separates low-mileage old cars from high-mileage young ones; requires vehicle_age or equivalent denominator.

features.name: pca_efficiency
features.justification: Combines correlated efficiency/mileage signals into orthogonal components, reducing multicollinearity for linear models.
features.pandas_code:
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
X = df[cols].fillna(df[cols].median())
pca = PCA(n_components=2)
comps = pca.fit_transform(X)
df[['pca_eff_1','pca_eff_2']] = comps

report_markdown:
## Summary
Proposed five derived features focused on price normalization, skew correction, age/usage, and efficiency compression.
